texto: For just a second, Lauren wonders if she’s going insane. But insane people don’t think they’re insane, everybody knows that. But...since that fact of insanity is common knowledge, doesn’t that mean an insane person could use it to convince themselves they’re not actually insane? Wouldn’t that make them even more insane?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Patrick Anderson Jr. que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “For just a second, Lauren wonders if she’s going insane. But insane people don’t think they’re in…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, humor, insanity, com foco em insanity e quarter-life-crisis.

texto: Lauren realizes right then that the prospect of being single—of recent events leading to a divorce and her being a single mother with child support checks and the like—scares her to death. Dating itself is such a frightening, vulnerable time period, no matter what the circumstances. It sucks, really. She doesn’t want to go through all that again.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Patrick Anderson Jr.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Lauren realizes right then that the prospect of being single—of recent events leading to a divorc…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, dating, humor pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e humor.
